title: NOC 21111 – Forestry Professionals | GTR Immigration
description: Forestry Professionals play a vital role in sustainable forest management, environmental conservation, wildfire prevention, and timber-resource development across Canada. Classified under NOC 21111 (TEER 1), this occupation is experiencing long-term national demand—making it a strong pathway for skilled immigrants through Express Entry–linked streams, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and employer-driven work permits.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Conduct forest surveys and resource assessments
Direct harvesting, silviculture, and reforestation programs
Negotiate forestry contracts and land-use agreements
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in forestry or forestry engineering
Two years of supervised practicum experience
Registration is required in nearly all provinces except Prince Edward Island and Manitoba.
job_outlook: Between 2022–2031, Canada is expected to create around 11,500 new forestry roles, while approximately 9,600 job seekers will enter the labour market—indicating favourable immigration prospects for internationally trained forestry specialists.
additional_information: Forestry Professionals are actively recruited across federal and provincial governments, consulting firms, forest-product companies, and academic institutions. Demand continues to grow as Canada invests in climate resilience, wildfire management, and sustainable resource development.
Forestry and lumber companiesEnvironmental consulting firmsProvincial and federal agenciesUniversities and research institutionsIndigenous-land management authoritiesPrivate woodlot operators
Annual salaries commonly range from $57,800 to $111,000 CAD, depending on experience, province, and certification status.
